fields.summary: Add support for different network setups when launching the docker container

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: We should allow users to be able to launch containers with appropriate network setups. For security, we should allow admins to provide a set of options that the users are allowed to use.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

comments.body:
Hi [~vvasudev],

Would you mind if I take over this JIRA if you are not working on this currently?

thanks,
-Sidharta

Hi, [~sidharta-s]
We are planning to add BRIDGE mode support for Docker containers. Wondering if you have already implemented this feature. Any progress?
Hi [~zyluo], I have work in progress patch - I should be able to put it up this week.
[~sidharta-s] Thanks for the heads up. We'd definitely try it out.
Uploaded a patch that supports configurable set of allowed networks and an additional configuration knob for a default to be used in case the incoming request doesn't specify a network to use.
[~vvasudev], I have submitted the patch to Jenkins. Could you please review the patch when possible? Thanks!

Thanks, [~vvasudev]. Uploaded a new patch with checkstyle fixes and the comment fixed. The test {{testContainerLaunchWithNetworkingDefaults}} doesn't seem to depend on test execution order - could you clarify what you mean here?

Thanks [~sidharta-s] for the patch and [~vvasudev]'s review!

When we use network type "bridge", it seems that we need add "-P" in docker run args to map container's exposed ports to host ports?

This message was automatically generated.

[~hex108],
Thanks for raise this port handling problem. We may not only support "- P " but also "-p hostPort(range):containerPort(range)".
Filed YARN-5168 to address it.
Yes, we faced same problems and solved it by "-p", we could discuss more in YARN-5168.

As discussed in YARN-5168, for host network, port mapping might conflict for each container. And some container might use important system port, e.g. port 80, 22. Maybe we could address it in following jira?

I would be nice, if using bridge mode, the admin can specify a port range in yarn-site.xml which is available on all NM hosts for port mapping.

Users should be restricted from mapping a container port to a specific host port, always a container port mapped to a random port among the available port range.

Thoughts?
Should we continue this discussion on YARN-5168 ?  I'll add my comments there as well.

[~sidharta-s] clarified that the tests have no ordering requirement in an offline conversation and he is correct. I also agree that the ports discussion should be handled in another JIRA - it's a far bigger change with things like collision and scheduling that need to thought through.

Latest patch looks good to me. I'll commit it tomorrow if no one objects.
Pushed to trunk and branch-2. Thanks [~sidharta-s]!

SUCCESS: Integrated in Hadoop-trunk-Commit #9885 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/9885/])
YARN-4007. Add support for different network setups when launching the (vvasudev: rev 86fb58b7dc832c2df30469d128598a6a1bed8df5)

This message was automatically generated.

[~hex108],
Thanks for raise this port handling problem. We may not only support "- P " but also "-p hostPort(range):containerPort(range)".
Filed YARN-5168 to address it.
Yes, we faced same problems and solved it by "-p", we could discuss more in YARN-5168.

As discussed in YARN-5168, for host network, port mapping might conflict for each container. And some container might use important system port, e.g. port 80, 22. Maybe we could address it in following jira?

I would be nice, if using bridge mode, the admin can specify a port range in yarn-site.xml which is available on all NM hosts for port mapping.

Users should be restricted from mapping a container port to a specific host port, always a container port mapped to a random port among the available port range.

Thoughts?
Should we continue this discussion on YARN-5168 ?  I'll add my comments there as well.

[~sidharta-s] clarified that the tests have no ordering requirement in an offline conversation and he is correct. I also agree that the ports discussion should be handled in another JIRA - it's a far bigger change with things like collision and scheduling that need to thought through.

Latest patch looks good to me. I'll commit it tomorrow if no one objects.
Pushed to trunk and branch-2. Thanks [~sidharta-s]!
